title: podostemum rutifolium subsp. ricciiforme
intro: Podostemum rutifolium subsp. ricciiforme, also known as Ricci's waterstretcher, is a rare and fascinating plant species belonging to the family Podostemaceae, which is a group of aquatic plants that grow on rocks in fast-flowing streams and rivers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Podostemum rutifolium subsp. ricciiforme include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
